messages: I worry social life will suffer if I start winding down earlier—most of my friends text late at night.
I sometimes wake up naturally in the middle of the night and have trouble returning to sleep; that's a core issue for me.
I love coffee in the afternoon; cutting it out would be a personal tragedy, but maybe I could tweak timing.
I read somewhere that getting outdoor light in the morning helps more than anything else - might be an easy habit to add.
My gym offers an early class at 6am, but getting there would be a stretch if I change everything.
I'm considering buying a dawn-simulating lamp but haven't decided; gadgets can be helpful but also pricey.
Okay, circling back to the schedule change I told you about earlier—can you map out a day-by-day transition plan to get me ready?
Spread it across the full interval I initially described, with small, steady shifts each day rather than big jumps.
For each day, give a target bedtime and wake time plus one small evening action and one small morning action to support that day's shift.
Keep the adjustments modest so I still get sufficient nightly rest, and avoid recommending pills or supplements.
If I fall behind one day, include a simple recovery step that doesn't require restarting the whole plan.
Number the days relative to the plan's start (Day 1, Day 2, etc.) instead of using calendar dates.
Begin with a one-sentence summary of the overall approach, then list the daily bullets.
Keep it concise and actionable—I'm looking for practical steps I can follow without a lot of extra reading.
If there are small tools or cues that help (like light or alarm tweaks), mention them briefly but don't list complicated equipment.
